lock_of_the_week: CLAUDE 4 COMMISH (-500) over Camp of the New Orleans Saints — Mais sha, dis da Lock of da week, save ya money for a fresh boudin link, that's true. CLAUDE da #1 power team at 142, da biggest gap on da whole board — dey lead da league in steals (100) AND strikeouts (959), tied for da win lead (61), and grade top-3 in TEN of da fourteen cats, I gar-on-tee. Camp cooked up 53 RBI to upset Caminero last week, dat's true, but one hot gumbo don't feed da whole boil — on da season dey 10th in average AND homers AND slugging, and da model don't project dem to win a single category clean. Pick: CLAUDE by way of a Commander's Palace turtle soup AND a second bowl, dat's true.

matchups.blurb: OOO WEE, a contenduh matchup — #5 against #3 — and Exxon Valdez is da favorite, that's true. Now dat we threw out da stale preseason recipe, here's da honest plate: Valdez leads da league in RBI (456), saves-plus-holds (76), AND K/BB (3.39), and sit 2nd in runs and OBP — dat's da real profile, sha, not da six phantom #1s da old bot was sellin'. Caminero ain't no slouch — 2nd in strikeouts (931), 3rd in wins (60) AND homers (141) — but da rate pitchin' been slippin' (staff ERA up from 3.81 to 4.32 da last month) and dey K/BB is 8th. Valdez's bullpen (1st in saves-plus-holds vs Caminero's 7th) and dat league-best K/BB tip da close cats. Pick: Exxon Valdez by way of a Galatoire's lunch dat runs clear til dinner, dat's true.

WOOOO DOGGY, da hottest team in da league against da saves-and-steals specialist, that's true. Spoons done climbed to #2, and dey pitchin' leads da whole CFBL in quality starts (66), ERA (3.27), AND WHIP (1.07) — and now da bats woke up too, 15 homers a week da last month after just 8 early. ShawSwank play dey game — 2nd in saves-plus-holds (75), 4th in steals (76) — but dey punt da power hard, bottom-three in average, homers, AND slugging. So ShawSwank gon' grab saves and steals and maybe a hold cat, dat's true, but Spoons take everything dat involve hittin' it hard or throwin' it past ya. Pick: Spoons by way of a shrimp étouffée so thick da spoon stand straight up, dat's true.

team_a.name: Camp of the New Orleans Saints

matchups.blurb: Mais oui, dis da LOCK OF DA WEEK, so save ya money for crawfish season, that's true. CLAUDE is da #1 power team at 142 — dey lead da league in steals (100) and strikeouts (959), tied for da win lead (61), and grade top-3 in ten of fourteen cats, da deepest roster da model ever seen. Camp had dey best week of da season to upset Caminero (53 RBI, sha!), and dat's true, but da season-long bats still rank 10th in average, homers, AND slugging. Da model don't see Camp winnin' a single category clean dis week — best dey do is tie wins and saves-plus-holds. Pick: CLAUDE 4 COMMISH by way of a long Brennan's brunch wit' eggs Hussarde AND bananas Foster, dat's true.

Sha, dis da closest game on da board — two big bats, two leaky pitchin' staffs, basically a pick'em, that's true. PO' BOYD leads da league in homers (149) AND slugging (.468) and hit 2nd in average (.264); Y&Y leads da league in average (.265), 3rd in slugging, 4th in runs. Da pitchin' is where it get ugly for both: PO' BOYD dead-last in K/BB and 10th in ERA — and trendin' WORSE, a 5.47 da last month — while Y&Y been quietly fixin' dey staff (ERA down to 3.75, WHIP down to 1.27). Dat recent pitchin' edge for Y&Y is why dis one a coin flip even though PO' BOYD got da scarier lineup. Pick: PO' BOYD by a whisker, like da last cold oyster at Casamento's, dat's true.

Cher, da battle of two teams tryin' to find dey way outta da swamp, that's true. Balls got da clear pitchin' edge — 2nd in quality starts (60), top-half in ERA AND WHIP — and dat's da difference here. Duran live on wins (tied-1st, 61) and speed (2nd in steals, 95), so dey gon' grab W and SB and maybe a quality-start cat, dat's true. But Duran's bats is da worst in da league — dead-last in homers, average, OBP, AND slugging — gettin' shut out in da power cats every single week. Balls play wit' one hand tied too (dead-last in saves-plus-holds, zero some weeks), but dey still da better roster today. Pick: Balls to da Wall by way of a chicken-and-andouille jambalaya, dat's true.

Mais sha, Señor Burns roll into dis one a heavy favorite, that's true. Burns lead da WHOLE league in runs (512) AND OBP (.357), and grade 3rd in saves-plus-holds, K/BB, quality starts, ERA, AND average — a deep, balanced club wit' no real hole 'cept power (11th in RBI, 8th in homers). Polar is 2-11 and it show: last in runs AND ERA, 11th in wins AND strikeouts, wit' only dey leftover power (top-6 in homers and slugging) and a few saves to lean on. Polar might steal homers, slugging, and a save cat, dat's true, but Burns take everything else clean. Pick: Señor Burns by way of a Sunday gumbo wit' file AND okra, dat's true.
